You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 48ffc6e2).

## Issue to Resolve

property-naming flags extension properties
The following code gets flagged with 0.49.1:

```kotlin
val Context.adsDelegateFactory @SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get() =
  applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```

> Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed (standard:property-naming)

I would not expect this, if you're adding a property via an extension and leveraging `get()` since the value can indeed change inside the implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

So when I remove `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")` it does not get flagged as expected. So there's something weird going on.
Indeed, the annotation causes the check to fail. Same happens with:
```
val Context.adsDelegateFactory private get() =
    applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```
Problem will either be resolved in `0.49.2` only when more pressing bugs need to be fixed. Otherwise it will be fixed in the next normal release.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 21→
 22→/**
 23→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 25→ */
 26→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 27→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28→    Rule.Experimental {
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    private fun visitProperty(
 40→        property: ASTNode,
 41→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42→    ) {
 43→        property
 44→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45→            ?.let { identifier ->
 46→                when {
 47→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49→                    }
 50→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52→                    }
 53→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57→                    }
 58→                    else -> {
 59→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60→                    }
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 66→        identifier: ASTNode,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        identifier
 70→            .text
 71→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 72→            ?.let {
 73→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 74→            }
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 78→        identifier: ASTNode,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        identifier
 82→            .text
 83→            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 84→            ?.let {
 85→                emit(
 86→                    identifier.startOffset,
 87→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 88→                    false,
 89→                )
 90→            }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 94→        identifier: ASTNode,
 95→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→    ) {
 97→        identifier
 98→            .text
 99→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
100→            ?.let {
101→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
106→        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
107→            ?.firstChildNode
108→            ?.text == "get"
109→
110→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
113→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
114→            ?.children()
115→            .orEmpty()
116→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
123→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
124→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
125→            containsValKeyword() &&
126→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
127→
128→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
129→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
130→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
131→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
132→            ?.let { identifier ->
133→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
134→            }
135→            ?: false
136→
137→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
138→        treeParent
139→            .children()
140→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
141→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
142→            .any { it.text == identifier }
143→
144→    private companion object {
145→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
146→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
147→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
148→    }
149→}
150→
151→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
152→

Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the problem:

The `hasCustomGetter()` function checks if the first child of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` is "get":

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.firstChildNode
        ?.text == "get"
```

However, when there's an annotation before `get()` like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get()`, the first child of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` is a `MODIFIER_LIST` (containing the annotation), not the "get" keyword directly.

Let me write a test to verify this understanding and then fix it:

Now I understand the issue. The problem is in the `hasCustomGetter()` function. When a property accessor has an annotation like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`, the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node is a `MODIFIER_LIST` (containing the annotation), not the `GET_KEYWORD` directly.

Let me check how the AST structure looks for a property accessor with an annotation. The fix should be to check if the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node contains a `GET_KEYWORD` child anywhere in its children, not just as the first child.

Let me look at the fix:

 26→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 27→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28→    Rule.Experimental {
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    private fun visitProperty(
 40→        property: ASTNode,
 41→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42→    ) {
 43→        property
 44→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45→            ?.let { identifier ->
 46→                when {
 47→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49→                    }
 50→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52→                    }
 53→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57→                    }
 58→                    else -> {
 59→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60→                    }
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→    }

content.text:
The fix has been successfully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary of the Fix

**Root Cause:**
The `hasCustomGetter()` function was checking if the first child of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node has text "get". However, when there's an annotation before the getter (like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get()`), the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node is a `MODIFIER_LIST` (containing the annotation), not the `GET_KEYWORD` token directly.

**The Fix:**
Changed the `hasCustomGetter()` function from:
```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.firstChildNode
        ?.text == "get"
```

To:
```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.children()
        ?.any { it.elementType == GET_KEYWORD } == true
```

This change:
1. Added import for `GET_KEYWORD` element type
2. Changed the check to look through ALL children of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node for a `GET_KEYWORD` element type, rather than just checking the first child

This properly detects custom getters even when they have annotations or visibility modifiers (like `private get()`).

**Tests:**
- All existing tests pass
- Created new test file `PropertyNamingRuleAnnotatedGetterTest.kt` with tests specifically for the reported issue cases:
  - Extension property with annotated getter
  - Extension property with private getter
  - Object val property with annotated getter
  - Object val property with private annotated getter
  - Top level val property with annotated getter

 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType

 22→/**
 23→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 25→ */
 26→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 27→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28→    Rule.Experimental {
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    private fun visitProperty(
 40→        property: ASTNode,
 41→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42→    ) {
 43→        property
 44→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45→            ?.let { identifier ->
 46→                when {
 47→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49→                    }
 50→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52→                    }
 53→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57→                    }
 58→                    else -> {
 59→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60→                    }
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 66→        identifier: ASTNode,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        identifier
 70→            .text
 71→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 72→            ?.let {
 73→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 74→            }
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 78→        identifier: ASTNode,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        identifier
 82→            .text
 83→            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 84→            ?.let {
 85→                emit(
 86→                    identifier.startOffset,
 87→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 88→                    false,
 89→                )
 90→            }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 94→        identifier: ASTNode,
 95→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→    ) {
 97→        identifier
 98→            .text
 99→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
100→            ?.let {
101→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
106→        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
107→            ?.firstChildNode
108→            ?.text == "get"
109→
110→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
113→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
114→            ?.children()
115→            .orEmpty()
116→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
123→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
124→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
125→            containsValKeyword() &&
126→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
127→
128→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
129→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
130→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
131→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
132→            ?.let { identifier ->
133→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
134→            }
135→            ?: false
136→
137→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
138→        treeParent
139→            .children()
140→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
141→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
142→            .any { it.text == identifier }
143→
144→    private companion object {
145→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
146→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
147→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
148→    }
149→}
150→
151→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId

  7→
  8→class PropertyNamingRuleTest {
  9→    private val propertyNamingRuleAssertThat =
 10→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { PropertyNamingRule() }
 11→
 12→    @Test
 13→    fun `Given a valid property name then do not emit`() {
 14→        val code =
 15→            """
 16→            var foo = "foo"
 17→            """.trimIndent()
 18→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 19→    }
 20→
 21→    @ParameterizedTest(name = ": {0}")
 22→    @ValueSource(
 23→        strings = [
 24→            "Foo",
 25→            "foo_bar",
 26→        ],
 27→    )
 28→    fun `Given an invalid property name then do emit`(propertyName: String) {
 29→        val code =
 30→            """
 31→            var $propertyName = "foo"
 32→            """.trimIndent()
 33→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 34→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case")
 35→    }
 36→
 37→    @Test
 38→    fun `Given a const property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 39→        val code =
 40→            """
 41→            const val foo = "foo"
 42→            const val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar-2"
 43→            const val ŸÈŠ_THÎS_IS_ALLOWED_123 = "Yes this is allowed"
 44→            """.trimIndent()
 45→        @Suppress("ktlint:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:max-line-length")
 46→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 47→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 11, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    @Test
 51→    fun `Given a top level val property name not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 52→        val code =
 53→            """
 54→            val foo = Foo()
 55→            val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 56→            """.trimIndent()
 57→        @Suppress("ktlint:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:max-line-length")
 58→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 59→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(1, 5, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    @Test
 63→    fun `Given an object val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do emit`() {
 64→        val code =
 65→            """
 66→            class Foo {
 67→                companion object {
 68→                    val foo = Foo()
 69→                    val FOO_BAR = FooBar()
 70→                }
 71→            }
 72→            """.trimIndent()
 73→        @Suppress("ktlint:argument-list-wrapping", "ktlint:max-line-length")
 74→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code)
 75→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(3, 13, "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed")
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    @Test
 79→    fun `Given an object override val property name not having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 80→        val code =
 81→            """
 82→            open class Foo {
 83→                open val foo = "foo"
 84→            }
 85→
 86→            val BAR = object : Foo() {
 87→                override val foo = "bar"
 88→            }
 89→            """.trimIndent()
 90→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    @Test
 94→    fun `Given an object val property name having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
 95→        val code =
 96→            """
 97→            class Foo {
 98→                companion object {
 99→                    val foo
100→                        get() = foobar() // Lint can not check whether data is immutable
101→                }
102→            }
103→            """.trimIndent()
104→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
105→    }
106→
107→    @Test
108→    fun `Given a backing val property name having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
109→        val code =
110→            """
111→            class Foo {
112→                private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
113→
114→                val elementList: List<Element>
115→                    get() = _elementList
116→            }
117→            """.trimIndent()
118→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
119→    }
120→
121→    @Test
122→    fun `Given a backing val property name containing diacritics having a custom get function and not in screaming case notation then do not emit`() {
123→        val code =
124→            """
125→            class Foo {
126→                private val _elementŁîšt = mutableListOf<Element>()
127→
128→                val elementŁîšt: List<Element>

131→            """.trimIndent()
132→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()

 22→/**
 23→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 25→ */
 26→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 27→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28→    Rule.Experimental {
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    private fun visitProperty(
 40→        property: ASTNode,
 41→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42→    ) {
 43→        property
 44→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45→            ?.let { identifier ->
 46→                when {
 47→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49→                    }
 50→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52→                    }
 53→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57→                    }
 58→                    else -> {
 59→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60→                    }
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 66→        identifier: ASTNode,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        identifier
 70→            .text
 71→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 72→            ?.let {
 73→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 74→            }
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 78→        identifier: ASTNode,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        identifier
 82→            .text
 83→            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 84→            ?.let {
 85→                emit(
 86→                    identifier.startOffset,
 87→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 88→                    false,
 89→                )
 90→            }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 94→        identifier: ASTNode,
 95→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→    ) {
 97→        identifier
 98→            .text
 99→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
100→            ?.let {
101→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
106→        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
107→            ?.firstChildNode
108→            ?.text == "get"
109→
110→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
113→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
114→            ?.children()
115→            .orEmpty()
116→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
123→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
124→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
125→            containsValKeyword() &&
126→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
127→
128→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
129→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
130→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
131→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
132→            ?.let { identifier ->
133→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
134→            }
135→            ?: false
136→
137→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
138→        treeParent
139→            .children()
140→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
141→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
142→            .any { it.text == identifier }
143→
144→    private companion object {
145→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
146→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
147→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
148→    }
149→}

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 85→            ?.let {
 86→                emit(
 87→                    identifier.startOffset,
 88→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 89→                    false,
 90→                )
 91→            }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 95→        identifier: ASTNode,
 96→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 97→    ) {
 98→        identifier
 99→            .text
100→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
101→            ?.let {
102→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
103→            }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
107→        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.any { it.elementType == GET_KEYWORD } == true
110→
111→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
114→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
115→            ?.children()
116→            .orEmpty()
117→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
118→
119→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
120→
121→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
124→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
125→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
126→            containsValKeyword() &&
127→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
130→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
131→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
132→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
133→            ?.let { identifier ->
134→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
135→            }
136→            ?: false
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
139→        treeParent
140→            .children()
141→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
142→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
143→            .any { it.text == identifier }
144→
145→    private companion object {
146→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
147→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
148→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
149→    }
150→}
151→
152→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
